rows.notes: Top-of-page title clearly written and centered. No monetary amounts on this line.
Pounds '60' is very clear in the right-most pounds column; shillings and pence shown as dashes (interpreted as 0). Vertical ruling lines are unambiguous here.
Amount '20' in pounds column is clear. The place name handwriting is faint/abbreviated and could read several ways; I transcribed it as 'Greenwich (?)' to reflect uncertainty in description.
Pounds '2' visible. The description is partially obscured and abbreviations ('1 yr') present; uncertain reading of the name, hence lower confidence.
Handwritten place name fairly legible as 'Bampton'. Amount reads '5' in pounds column and '10' in shillings; pence column blank (interpreted as 0). Column boundaries are clear.
Amount columns read as 16 (pounds), 13 (shillings), 4 (pence); description is in a looping script and could be 'Ashacote' or similar — uncertain spelling.
Description reads 'Jesus College' reasonably clearly. Amount shows '1' in pounds and '18' in shillings. Pence blank. Columns aligned well.
Place name 'Hertford' is legible. Pounds column shows '18'; shillings/pence blank (0).
Amount '8' pounds and '8' shillings are clear. The description seems to reference 'Barker' and 'St Martin' but the script is cramped and uncertain, so I noted a query in the description.
Pounds '6' is legible. The word looks like 'Captain' or a personal name; handwriting ambiguous.
Amounts read as 1 pound 10 shillings. The description is heavily abbreviated and the meaning of 'Company' and following marks is unclear; low confidence in text.
Pounds '6' legible. The description appears to be 'Milton' with 'do' (ditto) following; handwriting faint but plausible.
Amount '5' pounds, '4' shillings, '4' pence visible though small. The place name is deduced as 'Madenham Little Lew' from the script; moderate uncertainty in letters.
Monetary columns read '3' pounds, '4' shillings, '4' pence. The left-hand name is scribbled and could be 'Beck' or 'Beckford'; description uncertain.
Amounts read 1 lb 4 s. Left-hand description is abbreviated and faint; multiple plausible readings so lower confidence.
Pounds '2' clearly visible. Name is short and smudged — likely 'Lennard' or 'Lennardt' but uncertain.
This is a section heading in a larger script; no amounts on the heading line itself. The wording is reasonably clear.
Pounds '44' and shillings '11' are visible though the first digit '4' slightly slants; 'Hiddelington' and 'Rectory 2 yrs' read with moderate certainty.
Amount reads 4 pounds 19 shillings. 'Kidlington' or similar rectory appears but the first letter is faint; moderate confidence.
Pounds '56' is visible though the script of the place name is unclear (I transcribed as 'Llysham (?)'). Shillings/pence blank.
This line appears to be 'Do.' (ditto) with 'Vicarage' and amount reading 8 pounds 8 shillings. Handwriting abbreviated and faint.
Clear sectional heading in larger, underlined script. No amounts on heading.

Pounds '229' and shillings '16' read clearly. There is a tiny fractional mark in the pence column interpreted as three-quarters (0.75); the whole pence digit appears to be 0. The fraction glyph is faint but resembles '3q' or a loop indicating 3/4.
Amounts read 160 pounds, 4 shillings, 3 pence. Description appears to be a personal or place name 'J. Newington' with an abbreviation; handwriting of the suffix is uncertain.
Numbers in columns read as 8-16-7 but shillings 16 and pence 7 are slightly cramped and could be misread; the left description is faint and uncertain.
Pounds appear to read '91'; shillings '2' and pence '10' with a small half-penny symbol next to it (interpreted as 0.5). The pence+fraction reading is moderately uncertain due to small script.
Pounds '43' and shillings '9' are visible. There is a faint halfpenny mark in the pence column interpreted as 0.5. Description 'Langton Rectory' legible.
Amounts read 17-5-10 with a small mark after pence consistent with a halfpenny (0.5). Script for 'Chase Hill' is fairly clear.
Numbers show 48 pounds, 13 shillings, 9 pence and a small '3q' or three-quarter mark after the pence (interpreted as 0.75). The fraction glyph is faint which reduces confidence.
Amount columns read 8-13-1 with a small 3/4 mark in pence. The place name is uncertain; could be 'Garsington Almond' or similar — handwriting ambiguous.
Pounds and shillings read as 2 and 1; pence '6' is small but legible. Description is abbreviated and uncertain.
Columns show 21-13-11. The left-hand name is complex and unclear; likely two words with a surname 'Napier'; moderate to low confidence.

Numbers 8-1-4 visible though small. 'Pilton Ferry' is a plausible reading of the place name in the script.

Pounds '7' and shillings '5' clear. There is a very small farthing/quarter mark after pence interpreted as 0.25; whole pence seems blank (0). The fraction glyph is faint.
